title: Inheritance, MRO, Super, and Mixins · Program Defense
summary: Evaluate extension designs for physical, digital, and restricted library resources. Every item uses a fresh case and misconception-specific distractors. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for an extensible resource type hierarchy. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Use inheritance when a subtype preserves the semantic contract and reusable implementation of its base type.
Override methods while preserving input, output, state, exception, and side-effect expectations of callers.
Use zero-argument super for cooperative method chains and pass compatible arguments through participating classes.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:

steps.title: Diagnose the hidden failure
steps.instruction:
Use a changed quiz case derived from this module: python inheritance mro. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.

steps.title: Defend one difficult decision
steps.instruction: Write a concise evidence note that explains when Oop Composition Vs Inheritance applies, what can fail, and how the changed case was verified.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Choose composition, protocols, delegation, or inheritance by variability, substitutability, ownership, and change cost.
